title: 142  Why not chant and feel the personal presence of  Krishna
speaker: HH Stoka Krishna Maharaj

segments.text: This understanding that
Krishna is non-different from his holy name
Again, the person Krishna
and his name are non-different
So if Arjuna is missing the person Krishna
Somebody may ask the question, why doesn't he chant and experience the presence of the
person Krishna? Why does he do that?
Coming back to my original question which I started in the beginning of the class
What is the answer?
Non-different person and the name are non-different
and that is realized not in our conditioned state
but in the liberated state. Arjuna is already in the liberated platform
So why doesn't he chant
and experience the presence of the person Krishna
That's what we hear all the time
The person and the name are identical
This is the nature of the absolute truth
Krishna means, Krishna, his name, his paraphernalia
his abode, his qualities
his devotees
All are identical
Advaya Jnana
You cannot separate
Krishna from his name, Krishna from his abode, Krishna from his paraphernalia, Krishna from his qualities
You cannot separate
So why Arjuna is feeling separation?
Why Arjuna is feeling separation?
Why Arjuna is feeling separation?
Actually, Arjuna is remembering Krishna more intensely than when Krishna was present
That is what the previous chapter, sorry previous verse says
that with great difficulty he checked the tears of grief that smeared his eyes
He was distressed because Lord Krishna was out of his sight
and he increasingly felt affection for him
His affection for Krishna has increased
After Krishna's disappearance, his affection for Krishna has increased
So he is remembering Krishna more intensely
So there is no separation from Krishna
for somebody who intensely remembers Krishna
There is no question of separation
So this will be explained in the Bhaktiya Samrita Sindhu
that there are varieties of spiritual ecstasy
and the devotees in their dealings with Krishna
in their relationship with Krishna
in rendering service to Krishna
they experience varieties of spiritual ecstasy
and even in feeling separation from Krishna
like Arjuna is feeling
there is a certain spiritual ecstasy
These are all spiritual feelings
These are not to be confused with material feeling of separation or grief
Just like when somebody dies, near and dear one dies in the family
the other surviving descendants they feel grief due to separation
That's a material feeling
Now Krishna has disappeared
Arjuna is feeling separation
This is a spiritual feeling
This is the cause of a different kind of spiritual ecstasy
Just like Gopis
When Krishna disappeared from the arena of the Rasa dance
Gopis began to feel separation from Krishna
And they began to search for Krishna
They became almost mad searching for Krishna
Where are you?
